q: The burning wood in the air releases heat and _____ energies.
The firework displays illustrates a spectacular release of energy _____ in a chemical reaction.
Photosynthesis is the chemical reaction that occurs when plants use energy from the sun, _____ and water to synthesize the food that the plants need to survive.
Except for the energy produced from _____, all of man's energy resources are chemicals or involve the utilisation of energy through chemical reactions.
Whenever a chemical reaction (chemical change) occurs, energy is usually _____ to or from the surroundings.
Changing states of matter (physical change) also involve _____ changes.
_____ is the energy due to the movement of a body.
_____ is due to the position of a body.
Energy can change from one form to another without a net loss or gain of energy and it is called energy _____.
Electric motor can convert from electrical energy into _____ energy.
Hair dryer can convert _____ energy into thermal energy.
Battery can convert chemical energy into _____ energy.
Automobile engine can convert chemical energy into _____ energy.
Solar cell can convert _____ energy into electrical energy.
_____ energy is the energy that is stored in the bonds of chemical compounds such as atoms and molecules.
_____ energy is the source of most of the energy that humans need to function.
Humans release this chemical energy during _____ and use it to power systems inside the body.
Plants store chemical energy in carbohydrates formed during _____.
Whenever a chemical reaction takes place between reactants to form products, there is always an energy change usually in the form of _____.
_____ energy often involves changes in energy due to the breaking and formation of bonds.
Chemical reactions provide electricity which is the result of interactions between ions in an _____.
Iron rusting is _____.
When the ice melts at room temperature, that is an _____ process.
Examples of exothermic reaction is _____, the combustion of a fuel, the neutralisation reaction, and rusting of iron.
In an exothermic process, the energy of the product is _____ the energy of the reactants.
Examples of endothermic reaction are melting of ice, _____, evaporating liquid water, thermal decomposition of limestone.
a: mixing of sulphuric acid and water

q: In an endothermic process, the energy of the product is _____ the energy of the reactants.
The total _____ energy of a substance is called its enthalpy (or heat content).
The change in enthalpy describes the amount of heat transferred at constant _____ during a chemical reaction.
The heat absorbed or released in a process occurring at constant pressure is called the _____ change.
For an _____ reaction where energy is absorbed from the surroundings, the total energy of the products is greater than that of reactants.

Standard conditions chosen for energy changes are _____ and 1 atm pressure.
The standard enthalpy change of formation of a compound is the enthalpy change when one mole of a compound is formed from its _____ under standard conditions.
The standard enthalpy change of combustion is always _____.
The standard enthalpy change of _____ is the enthalpy change when solutions of an acid and an alkali react together under standard conditions to produce one mole of water.
The numerical value of heat of neutralisation is _____.
A calorimeter is a device to measure the amount of heat released or absorbed in reactions that occur in _____.
The amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of _____ of water by one degree is known as the specific heat capacity of water.
The higher the bond enthalpy, the _____ the bond to break it.
The enthalpy change of _____ is the heat change that occurs when one mole of a substance is dissolved in water.
The bond breaking is a/an _____ process.
Standard enthalpy change is measured at _____ and 760 mmHg.
Combustion of fuels is (a/an) _____ process.
When the energy needed to break bonds is _____ the energy needed to form bonds, the reaction is exothermic.
